Cuvées

Fleurie

4.8
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Low tannin · High acidity · Elegant · Romantic

Domaine Yvon MetrasBeaujolais€€€€€€€Still Red

Natural Fleurie from Yvon Metras, with the appellation's signature pink granite-driven floral perfume. Zero sulfur and whole-cluster produce remarkable aromatic transparency.

Cuvée page →

Fleurie Les Moriers

4.6
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Low tannin · High acidity · Elegant · Rustic

Domaine ChignardBeaujolais€€€€€€€Still Red

Historic Fleurie single-vineyard from Les Moriers, one of Chignard's flagship parcels on pink granite. Perfumed, floral and classically structured.

Cuvée page →

Morgon

4.8
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Low tannin · High acidity · Easygoing · Rustic

Domaine Marcel LapierreBeaujolais€€€€€€€Still Red

Whole-cluster Gamay fermented with native yeasts, zero sulfur. Transparent, mineral Morgon with lifted red fruit and the characteristic schist-iron signature of the appellation.

Cuvée page →

Morgon Cote du Py

4.9
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Firm tannin · High acidity · Contemplative · Bold

Domaine Jean FoillardBeaujolais€€€€€€€Still Red

Cote du Py Gamay from the volcanic-schist hill at the heart of Morgon. Dense, structured, with high acidity and an iron-mineral finish that rewards cellaring.

Cuvée page →

Moulin-a-Vent

4.6
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · High acidity · Bold · Contemplative

Chateau du Moulin-a-VentBeaujolais€€€€€€€Still Red

Estate Moulin-a-Vent from the chateau adjacent to the appellation's iconic windmill. Dense, structured Gamay from granite and manganese soils; one of the cru's more powerful expressions.

Cuvée page →

Moulin-a-Vent Couvent des Thorins

4.8
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Bold · Elegant

Chateau du Moulin-a-VentBeaujolais€€€€€€€€Still Red
Cuvée page →

Chateau Angelus

4.9
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · Medium acidity · Bold · Elegant

Chateau AngelusBordeaux€€€€€€€€€€Still Red

Merlot and Cabernet Franc from 27 classified hectares at the foot of Saint-Emilion's slope. Generous, opulent and structured, with dark cherry, violets and cedar complexity; certified organic from the 2021 vintage.

Cuvée page →

Chateau Ausone

5.0
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · High acidity · Contemplative · Elegant

Chateau AusoneBordeaux€€€€€€€€€€Still Red

From just 7 hectares of steep limestone slopes, Ausone is a wine of concentration, precision and mineral tension. Cabernet Franc dominates, giving iron-rich structure, dried-flower fragrance and an extraordinary seamless finish.

Cuvée page →

Chateau Cheval Blanc

5.0
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · Medium acidity · Contemplative · Elegant

Chateau Cheval BlancBordeaux€€€€€€€€€€Still Red

Cabernet Franc-dominant Saint-Emilion of breathtaking silkiness: violets and graphite on the nose, an opulent yet precise palate of black cherry and tobacco, seamless tannin structure and a finish of uncommon length.

Cuvée page →

Chateau Clinet

4.6
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · High acidity · Contemplative · Bold

Chateau ClinetBordeaux€€€€€€€€€Still Red

Merlot-dominant Pomerol with an unusually significant proportion of Cabernet Sauvignon giving structured backbone. Rich and concentrated with cassis, graphite, and leather; requires a minimum of 8-12 years in good vintages.

Cuvée page →

Chateau Cos d'Estournel

4.8
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · High acidity · Bold · Elegant

Chateau Cos d'EstournelBordeaux$$$$$$$$$Still Red

The most distinctive of the Saint-Estephe grands crus classés, with an unmistakable oriental spice and incense aromatic signature absent in Pauillac wines. Merlot-rich for Saint-Estephe, delivering seductive suppleness alongside the appellation's characteristic mineral-tannic power.

Cuvée page →

Chateau Figeac

4.9
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · High acidity · Contemplative · Elegant

Chateau FigeacBordeaux€€€€€€€€€€Still Red

The most Cabernet-dominant Right Bank premier cru in Bordeaux: near-equal thirds of Cabernet Franc, Cabernet Sauvignon and Merlot. Promoted to Premier Grand Cru Classe A in 2022. Combines peony and iris fragrance with electric energy, grainy Cabernet tannin and remarkable mineral length.

Cuvée page →

Chateau Haut-Brion

5.0
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · Medium acidity · Contemplative · Elegant

Chateau Haut-BrionBordeaux€€€€€€€€€€Still Red

Merlot-led first-growth Pessac-Leognan red combining cassis and graphite with earthy tobacco and cedar. Velvety tannins and exceptional length set it apart from Medoc peers.

Cuvée page →

Chateau Haut-Brion Blanc

4.9
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · High acidity · Elegant · Contemplative

Chateau Haut-BrionBordeaux€€€€€€€€€€Still White

One of the world's rarest and most sought-after dry whites, Haut-Brion Blanc blends Semillon richness with Sauvignon Blanc precision. White peach, orange blossom, and mineral salinity build to a honeyed, lingering finish.

Cuvée page →

Chateau La Mission Haut-Brion

4.9
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · Medium acidity · Contemplative · Bold

Chateau La Mission Haut-BrionBordeaux€€€€€€€€€€Still Red

La Mission Haut-Brion combines the gravel terroir of Talence with intense, brooding dark fruit. Blackberry, scorched earth, and graphite underpin a structured, powerful palate with velvety tannins and a finish that rivals the great first growths.

Cuvée page →

Chateau La Mission Haut-Brion Blanc

4.8
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · High acidity · Elegant · Contemplative

Chateau La Mission Haut-BrionBordeaux€€€€€€€€€€Still White

Originally sold as Laville Haut-Brion until 2009, this is among the most complex dry whites of Bordeaux. Semillon richness and Sauvignon Blanc freshness fuse with 100% new oak to produce a wine of extraordinary mineral precision and creamy depth.

Cuvée page →

Chateau Lafite Rothschild

4.9
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · High acidity · Contemplative · Elegant

Chateau Lafite RothschildBordeaux$$$$$$$$$$Still Red

The archetype of Pauillac first-growth: Cabernet-dominant, with penetrating lead pencil and cassis aromatics, precise fine-grained tannins, and a finish that evolves over decades.

Cuvée page →

Chateau Lafleur

4.9
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · High acidity · Contemplative · Elegant

Chateau LafleurBordeaux€€€€€€€€€€Still Red

Unique Pomerol with an equal Cabernet Franc-Merlot split giving a perfumed floral lift and structural precision rarely seen in the appellation. Biodynamic farming since 2014; produced from just 4.5 hectares on the Pomerol plateau.

Cuvée page →

Chateau Latour

4.9
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · High tannin · High acidity · Contemplative · Bold

Chateau LatourBordeaux$$$$$$$$$$Still Red

The most structured and austere of the Pauillac first-growths. The L'Enclos parcel of old-vine Cabernet Sauvignon delivers extraordinary concentration and mineral tension; legendary longevity requires patience of decades.

Cuvée page →

Chateau Lynch-Bages

4.7
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · High acidity · Bold · Rustic

Chateau Lynch-BagesBordeaux$$$$$$$$$Still Red

Lynch-Bages consistently overperforms its Fifth Growth classification. A richer, more extrovert Pauillac than the first-growths, with cassis generosity, smoky tobacco, and a robust structure that ages beautifully over 20-30 years.

Cuvée page →

Chateau Mouton Rothschild

4.9
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · High acidity · Bold · Contemplative

Chateau Mouton RothschildBordeaux$$$$$$$$$$Still Red

The most opulent of the Pauillac first-growths; Mouton Rothschild emphasises blackcurrant concentration, dark chocolate, and an extrovert aromatic generosity that sets it apart from the pencil-mineral restraint of Lafite and Latour.

Cuvée page →

Chateau Pape Clement

4.7
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · Medium acidity · Contemplative · Bold

Chateau Pape ClementBordeaux€€€€€€€€Still Red

From the oldest wine estate in Bordeaux, tracing its history to the 14th-century papacy. Cabernet-and-Merlot blend delivering cassis, graphite, and tobacco with flawless tannins; in great vintages approaches first-growth complexity.

Cuvée page →

Chateau Pavie

4.9
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · Medium acidity · Bold · Contemplative

Chateau PavieBordeaux€€€€€€€€€€Still Red

From 42 hectares at Saint-Emilion's highest point (110m), Pavie delivers concentrated, structured Merlot-dominant blends with extraordinary mineral depth. Pairs elegance with considerable power; requires minimum 15 years to fully integrate.

Cuvée page →

Chateau Pichon Longueville Comtesse de Lalande

4.7
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · High acidity · Elegant · Contemplative

Chateau Pichon Longueville Comtesse de LalandeBordeaux$$$$$$$$$Still Red

Known for its silky texture and unusually high 32% Merlot for a Left Bank estate, Pichon Lalande bridges the power of Pauillac with a suppleness and perfume reminiscent of Margaux. Louis Roederer ownership since 2006 has brought further precision.

Cuvée page →

Chateau Pichon-Longueville Baron

4.7
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · High acidity · Bold · Elegant

Chateau Pichon-Longueville BaronBordeaux$$$$$$$$$Still Red

Pauillac second-growth with an unusually high Cabernet Sauvignon proportion for the appellation. AXA Millesimes' investment since 1987 has lifted consistency dramatically; the 2022 vintage drew especially strong critical praise in bottle.

Cuvée page →

Chateau Pontet-Canet

4.8
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · High acidity · Bold · Contemplative

Chateau Pontet-CanetBordeaux$$$$$$$$$Still Red

The leading biodynamic estate in Bordeaux and the only Fifth Growth that regularly receives 100-point scores. Demeter-certified since 2014; unique use of amphorae alongside barrique ageing. The wines combine Pauillac's classical structure with a biodynamic freshness and earth energy not found elsewhere in the Medoc.

Cuvée page →

Chateau Rieussec

4.7
★★★★★★★★★★

Dessert · Full-bodied · High acidity · Elegant · Contemplative

Chateau RieussecBordeaux€€€€€€€€Dessert Noble Rot

One of the most consistent Premier Cru Classe Sauternes, with Rieussec's distinctive high Semillon content yielding a wine of great concentration and rich tropical fruit. Honey-drenched pineapple, orange, mango, and caramel with vibrant acidity and a very long finish.

Cuvée page →

Chateau Smith Haut Lafitte Rouge

4.7
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · Medium acidity · Contemplative · Bold

Chateau Smith Haut LafitteBordeaux€€€€€€€€€Still Red

A benchmark Pessac-Leognan Cabernet since the Cathiard family's acquisition in 1990. Cassis, graphite, and tobacco define a precise, structured palate; 60% new oak ageing adds cedar and spice. Consistent 97-98 point scores in recent vintages.

Cuvée page →

Chateau d'Yquem

5.0
★★★★★★★★★★

Dessert · Full-bodied · High acidity · Contemplative · Elegant

Chateau d'YquemBordeaux€€€€€€€€€€Dessert Noble Rot

The world's most celebrated botrytised sweet wine; the sole Premier Cru Superieur in the 1855 Sauternes classification. Semillon-dominant with intense noble-rot character: beeswax, saffron, apricot, and honey balanced by electric acidity. Ages for decades and improves for 50 or more years in great vintages.

Cuvée page →

Château Ducru-Beaucaillou

4.8
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · High acidity · Contemplative · Elegant

Château Ducru-BeaucaillouBordeaux€€€€€€€€€€Still Red

Archetype of refined Saint-Julien: cassis and dark cherry on a bed of Gunzian gravel minerality, with perfectly integrated silky tannins and an exceptionally lengthy finish.

Cuvée page →

Château Léoville Barton

4.7
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · High acidity · Contemplative · Elegant

Château Léoville BartonBordeaux€€€€€€€€€Still Red

Timeless Saint-Julien classic: a Cabernet-dominant blend with classic Médoc pencil shavings, licorice and cassis, built for patience with rich powdery tannins.

Cuvée page →

Château Margaux

5.0
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · High tannin · High acidity · Contemplative · Romantic

Château MargauxBordeaux€€€€€€€€€€Still Red

The most perfumed of the Médoc first growths: haunting floral aromatics of roses and violets above a core of concentrated cassis, extraordinary saline minerality, and tannins demanding decades of patience.

Cuvée page →

Château Palmer

4.9
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · High acidity · Contemplative · Elegant

Château PalmerBordeaux€€€€€€€€€€Still Red

Classified Third Growth performing at first-growth level: extraordinary richness from an unusually high Merlot proportion, with Thomas Duroux's biodynamic conversion delivering added aromatic complexity and freshness.

Cuvée page →

Grand Vin de Léoville du Marquis de Las Cases

4.9
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · High tannin · High acidity · Contemplative · Elegant

Château Léoville-Las CasesBordeaux€€€€€€€€€€Still Red

One of the great super-seconds of Bordeaux: extraordinarily concentrated cassis fruit with almost first-growth precision, dense tannins demanding patient cellaring of 15 or more years.

Cuvée page →

Hommage a Elisabeth Bouchet

4.9
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · High acidity · Contemplative · Elegant

Chateau AngelusBordeaux€€€€€€€€€€Still Red

100% Cabernet Franc from two old-vine parcels aged 65-100 years at the pied de cote. Produced in tiny quantities (5-9 barriques) only in exceptional vintages, this is a monument to the iron-mineral potential of old Cabernet Franc on Saint-Emilion limestone.

Cuvée page →

Le Pin

4.9
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · Medium acidity · Contemplative · Elegant

Le PinBordeaux€€€€€€€€€€Still Red

Micro-estate garagiste Pomerol with supremely hedonistic Merlot character: mocha-laced plum and violet aromatics, a voluptuous, fleshy palate, and remarkable concentration from just 400-600 cases per year.

Cuvée page →

Pavillon Blanc du Château Margaux

4.8
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Racy acidity · Elegant · Refreshing

Château MargauxBordeaux€€€€€€€€€€Still White

A rare Sauvignon Blanc white Bordeaux of extraordinary mineral precision: gunsmoke, slate and saline drive a taut citrus frame that ages beautifully over a decade or more.

Cuvée page →

Pavillon Rouge du Château Margaux

4.5
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · High acidity · Elegant · Romantic

Château MargauxBordeaux€€€€€€€€€Still Red

Exquisite second wine of the most famous first growth, sharing the same subtle power and floral finesse as the grand vin while offering earlier accessibility.

Cuvée page →

Petrus

5.0
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · Medium acidity · Contemplative · Elegant

PetrusBordeaux€€€€€€€€€€Still Red

100% Merlot from 11.4 ha of blue clay at Pomerol's centre. Silk-and-velvet texture with truffle, chocolate, and dark cherry at its core; structurally imposing yet hedonistic, with a finish that stretches for minutes.

Cuvée page →

Vieux Chateau Certan

4.8
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · High acidity · Contemplative · Elegant

Vieux Chateau CertanBordeaux€€€€€€€€€€Still Red

Pomerol's most Cabernet-Franc-inflected grand vin: 30% Cabernet Franc gives an aromatic lift and structural precision that sets VCC apart from Merlot-dominant peers. Sensuous and silky in great vintages, with decades of ageing potential.

Cuvée page →

Y de Yquem

4.6
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · High acidity · Contemplative · Elegant

Chateau d'YquemBordeaux€€€€€€€€€€Still White

The singular dry white wine of Chateau d'Yquem, made only in years when the estate decides against a botrytised grand vin or in addition to it. Sauvignon Blanc-dominant, barrel-fermented in 50% new oak, offering honeyed citrus, white peach, and mineral depth unique to the Sauternes terroir.

Cuvée page →

Dry · Full-bodied · High acidity · Elegant

Domaine LeflaiveBurgundy€€€€€€€€€€Still White

A richer, broader grand cru with citrus, honey and hazelnut over a saline, powerful frame.

Dry · Full-bodied · High acidity · Elegant

Domaine Etienne SauzetBurgundy€€€€€€€€€€Still White

A powerful, saline Batard with citrus, honey and hazelnut over a tightly wound mineral frame.

Dry · Full-bodied · High acidity · Elegant

Domaine RamonetBurgundy€€€€€€€€€€Still White

A powerful, broad Batard with citrus, honey and hazelnut over a saline, mineral frame.

The most collected Chablis grand cru: intensely mineral and saline, with citrus, oyster shell and an endless finish.

★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Racy acidity · Contemplative · Elegant

Champagne SalonChampagne€€€€€€€€€€Sparkling Traditional

One of Champagne's most mystical wines, made only in declared years from a single Grand Cru village: citrus and white flowers over iodine and chalk, with crystalline, racy intensity and extraordinary length.

Champagne TaittingerChampagne€€€€€€€€€Sparkling Traditional

One of the great vintage Blanc de Blancs, made only from Grand Cru Cote des Blancs Chardonnay: citrus and white flowers over chalk, with creamy texture and saline length.

The prestige rose counterpart to Comtes: red berries and blood orange over rose and spice, the palate silky with raspberry, chalk and a long finish.

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · High acidity · Elegant · Contemplative

GajaPiedmont€€€€€€€€€€Still Red

A south-facing cru above Costa Russi, Sori Tildin is the smoky, elegantly austere face of Gaja's Barbaresco: graphite, licorice and firm yet polished tannin over a refreshing spine of acidity.

Cuvée page →

Gaja Sperss

4.9
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · High acidity · Contemplative · Bold

GajaPiedmont€€€€€€€€€€Still Red

Gaja's Serralunga Barolo from the Marenca-Rivette parcels: deep, mineral and austere with sour black cherry, truffle and a decidedly salty, stony finish.

Cuvée page →

Giacomo Conterno Barolo Cascina Francia

4.9
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · High acidity · Contemplative · Elegant

Giacomo ConternoPiedmont€€€€€€€€€€Still Red

The benchmark traditional Serralunga Barolo, from the estate's own Cascina Francia vineyard, against which many producers measure their work: structured, savoury and built for decades.

Cuvée page →

Giacomo Conterno Barolo Riserva Monfortino

5.0
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · High acidity · Contemplative · Bold

Giacomo ConternoPiedmont€€€€€€€€€€Still Red

The summit of traditional Barolo, made only in great vintages from the Cascina Francia and Arione vineyards in Serralunga, with wild-swing fermentation and six to eight years in large old barrel before release.

Cuvée page →

Giuseppe Mascarello Barolo Monprivato

4.8
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · High acidity · Elegant · Contemplative

Giuseppe Mascarello e FiglioPiedmont€€€€€€€€€Still Red

The Monprivato cru in Castiglione Falletto, a near-monopole of Giuseppe Mascarello, gives a benchmark traditional Barolo of perfume, finesse and remarkable longevity.

Cuvée page →

La Scolca Gavi dei Gavi Etichetta Nera

4.6
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · High acidity · Elegant · Refreshing

La ScolcaPiedmont€€€€€€€€Still White

The Black Label flagship that defined premium Gavi, a sophisticated 100% Cortese of elegant structure, freshness and intense aromatic complexity that ages remarkably well.

Luciano Sandrone Barolo Aleste

★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · High acidity · Contemplative · Elegant

Luciano SandronePiedmont€€€€€€€€€Still Red

Sandrone's flagship from the Cannubi Boschis cru, renamed Aleste from the 2013 vintage, a polished, modern-classic Barolo of perfume and precision.

From one of Serralunga's greatest crus, Massolino's Vigna Rionda Riserva is a powerful, structured traditional Barolo released only after long ageing.

Roussanne-dominant white Chateauneuf with a lush, waxy texture, evolved aromas of beeswax and orchard fruit, and a mineral saline finish that ages magnificently.

Delas's La Landonne from the Cote Brune, a powerful and mineral expression of the famous lieu-dit. The Delas version is known for its density and iron character typical of the schist-iron soils.

★★★★★★★★★★

M. ChapoutierRhone Valley€€€€€€€€€€Still Red

The estate's flagship from the Ygay vineyard, released only in exceptional years after a decade-plus of ageing; the 2010 was named Wine Spectator Wine of the Year 2020, a savoury, classical Gran Reserva of great longevity.

La Rioja Alta S.A.Rioja€€€€€€€€Still Red

Spain's most celebrated single-vineyard Tempranillo, from a tiny old-vine plot at Laguardia; intense yet ethereal, with floral lift, silky tannins and profound minerality.

R. Lopez de Heredia Vina TondoniaRioja€€€€€€€€Still White

A barrel-aged white Rioja with cult status: deeply oxidative, nutty and waxy, with piercing acidity that keeps it electric for decades, often compared to white Burgundy and old Hunter Semillon.

Vina Tondonia Gran Reserva

★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · High acidity · Contemplative · Elegant

R. Lopez de Heredia Vina TondoniaRioja€€€€€€€€€Still Red

Released only in great years and only after a decade-plus in the cellar; ethereal, savoury and impossibly fresh, the Gran Reserva is one of the wine world's great old-style reds.

Vina Tondonia Reserva

4.9
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · High acidity · Contemplative · Elegant

R. Lopez de Heredia Vina TondoniaRioja€€€€€€€€Still Red

The reference traditional Rioja Reserva: pale brick colour, oxidative dried-fruit and balsamic aromatics over a fine-boned, high-acid frame that drinks like old Burgundy.

Vina Tondonia Rosado Gran Reserva

★★★★★★★★★★

R. Lopez de Heredia Vina TondoniaRioja€€€€€€€€Still Rose

The most famous rosado in the world: barrel-aged for years, copper-coloured, savoury and saline, it drinks like a serious gastronomic white rather than a summer pink.

Benanti ViticoltoriSicily€€€€€€€€Still White

The benchmark Etna Bianco Superiore from Milo: Carricante of crystalline mineral purity, volcanic salinity, and piercing acidity that rewards 8-plus years of cellaring.

★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Firm tannin · High acidity · Contemplative · Rustic

Benanti ViticoltoriSicily€€€€€€€€Still Red

From Rovittello on the north slope, this Benanti red shows the classic Etna paradox: light ruby colour but profound mineral depth, firm tannins and soaring acidity demanding patient cellaring.

Benanti Serra della Contessa

4.8
★★★★★★★★★★

Benanti ViticoltoriSicily€€€€€€€€Still Red

Benanti's top-tier Etna Rosso from the Serra della Contessa contrada at 700 metres: concentrated, structured, and among the most age-worthy expressions of Nerello Mascalese on the volcano.

COSSicily€€€€€€€Still Red

Sicily's most celebrated DOCG in its defining expression: Nero d'Avola structure lifted by Frappato's sour-cherry acidity, earthy-mineral finish from Vittoria clay-limestone soils.

COS Pithos Bianco

COS PithosSicily€€€€€€€Orange

Grecanico fermented on skins in amphora: a paradigmatic skin-contact white of honeyed stone-fruit richness, firm phenolic texture and saline-mineral finish.

COS Pithos Rosso

4.8
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Firm tannin · High acidity · Contemplative · Funky

COS PithosSicily€€€€€€€Orange

Nero d'Avola fermented and aged in terracotta amphorae without oak: the clay vessel imparts earthy minerality and textural grip unique among Sicilian reds.

Cusumano Sagana

4.5
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · Medium acidity · Bold · Contemplative

CusumanoSicily€€€€€€€Still Red

Sagana is Cusumano's prestige single-vineyard Nero d'Avola from Riesi in central Sicily: opulent, concentrated and barrique-treated to produce a richly chocolate-dark wine of considerable structure.

Cuvée page →

Donnafugata Anthilia

4.2
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Low tannin · High acidity · Refreshing · Easygoing

DonnafugataSicily€€€€€€Still White

Anthilia is Donnafugata's flagship entry white: Catarratto and Grecanico from the Contessa Entellina estate in a fresh, floral and mineral style that has defined quality Sicilian white wine for three decades.

Cuvée page →

Donnafugata Ben Rye Passito di Pantelleria

4.9
★★★★★★★★★★

Sweet · Full-bodied · Low tannin · Medium acidity · Festive · Romantic

Donnafugata PantelleriaSicily€€€€€€€€Dessert Passito

Ben Rye (Arabic for 'son of the wind') is Italy's most celebrated Passito di Pantelleria: sun-dried Zibibbo from the alberello pantesco vines on the volcanic island, achieving a concentration and aromatic complexity that has defined the appellation's international reputation.

Cuvée page →

Donnafugata Mille e Una Notte

4.8
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · Medium acidity · Contemplative · Elegant

DonnafugataSicily€€€€€€€€Still Red

Donnafugata's flagship red, Mille e Una Notte blends Nero d'Avola with Petit Verdot and Syrah from the Contessa Entellina estate: concentrated, structured and age-worthy with exceptional depth.

Cuvée page →

Donnafugata Tancredi

4.4
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · Medium acidity · Elegant · Bold

DonnafugataSicily€€€€€€€Still Red

Tancredi blends the warmth of Nero d'Avola with Cabernet Sauvignon's structural backbone, producing a Sicilian-Bordeaux hybrid with cedar-cedar depth and concentrated fruit aged gracefully in French barriques.

Cuvée page →

Duca di Salaparuta Bianca di Valguarnera

4.5
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Low tannin · Medium acidity · Elegant · Savoury

Duca di Salaparuta (Corvo)Sicily€€€€€€€Still White

Bianca di Valguarnera is Sicily's most celebrated Inzolia: Duca di Salaparuta's partial barrique treatment of the native grape produces a rich, nutty, mineral white with the depth to age several years.

Cuvée page →

Duca di Salaparuta Duca Enrico

4.6
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · Medium acidity · Contemplative · Bold

Duca di Salaparuta (Corvo)Sicily€€€€€€€€Still Red

Duca Enrico was Sicily's first landmark 100% Nero d'Avola: barrique-aged, structured and concentrated, it defined the modern Sicilian red wine archetype and remains a pillar of the island's wine history.

Cuvée page →

Feudo Montoni Vrucara

4.8
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · High tannin · High acidity · Contemplative · Bold

Feudo MontoniSicily€€€€€€€€Still Red

Vrucara is from Feudo Montoni's oldest pre-phylloxera Nero d'Avola vines in the high plateau of central Sicily: austere, iron-mineral, demanding years in bottle to reveal its full complexity.

Cuvée page →

Florio Marsala Vergine Riserva

4.8
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Low tannin · Medium acidity · Contemplative · Elegant

Cantine FlorioSicily€€€€€€€Fortified Marsala

Florio's Marsala Vergine Riserva is a benchmark for the driest and most complex Marsala style: fortified without added must, aged minimum 10 years in large oak in the tuff-stone cellars, developing deep rancio oxidative complexity.

Cuvée page →

Florio Targa Riserva 1840

4.7
★★★★★★★★★★

Medium Sweet · Full-bodied · Low tannin · Medium acidity · Festive · Contemplative

Cantine FlorioSicily€€€€€€€Fortified Marsala

The Targa Riserva 1840 is Florio's ambassador wine: a Marsala Superiore Riserva of medium-sweet richness, aged 5-plus years in the historic cellars, balancing caramelised dried-fruit complexity with the freshness of Grillo.

Cuvée page →

Frank Cornelissen Magma

4.9
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Firm tannin · High acidity · Contemplative · Funky

Frank CornelissenSicily€€€€€€€€€€Still Red

Magma is one of Italy's most sought-after cult wines: pure Nerello Mascalese from ungrafted pre-phylloxera vines above 1,000 metres, no sulphur added, aged in amphora. The wine embodies volcanic terroir at its most extreme and unfiltered.

Cuvée page →

Girolamo Russo 'A Rina

4.9
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Firm tannin · High acidity · Contemplative · Bold

Girolamo RussoSicily€€€€€€€€Still Red

The prestige Russo single-vineyard: 'A Rina (dialect for 'la rena' - the sand) from some of the deepest sandy volcanic soils on the north slope, producing the most austere and age-demanding wine in the portfolio.

Cuvée page →

Graci Arcuria

4.8
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Firm tannin · High acidity · Contemplative · Elegant

GraciSicily€€€€€€€€Still Red

Arcuria is the Graci flagship: from 52 acres of ungrafted pre-phylloxera vines on the north slope, it epitomises Etna's Burgundian paradox - light ruby colour belying deep mineral complexity and firm, precise tannins.

Cuvée page →

Gulfi Nerosanlore

4.7
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · Medium acidity · Contemplative · Elegant

GulfiSicily€€€€€€€€Still Red

Sanlorenzo is the most elegant of Gulfi's quartet: the highest-altitude contrada delivers a more refined palate structure with exceptional aromatic complexity.

Cuvée page →

Hauner Malvasia delle Lipari Passito

4.8
★★★★★★★★★★

Sweet · Full-bodied · Low tannin · Medium acidity · Festive · Romantic

Hauner (Malvasia delle Lipari)Sicily€€€€€€€€Dessert Passito

Hauner's Malvasia delle Lipari Passito is the benchmark for this rare Aeolian appellation: sun-dried Malvasia di Lipari producing a wine of extraordinary floral complexity, apricot sweetness and the distinctive volcanic mineral character of the island of Salina.

Cuvée page →

Marco De Bartoli Bukkuram Sole d'Agosto

4.9
★★★★★★★★★★

Sweet · Full-bodied · Low tannin · Medium acidity · Contemplative · Funky

Marco De Bartoli - Bukkuram (Pantelleria)Sicily€€€€€€€€Dessert Passito

Sole d'Agosto (August sun) is the De Bartoli minimal-intervention take on Passito di Pantelleria: zero-sulphur Zibibbo from the Bukkuram estate, producing an almost overwhelmingly concentrated dried-fruit and honey wine with a volcanic mineral dimension absent from other Pantelleria passiti.

Cuvée page →

Marco De Bartoli Vecchio Samperi

5.0
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Low tannin · Medium acidity · Contemplative · Funky

Marco De BartoliSicily€€€€€€€€Fortified Marsala

Vecchio Samperi is Marco De Bartoli's life work and Sicily's most important wine: un-fortified perpetuo Grillo aged in a 5-tier solera at Samperi, achieving Marsala-like depth and complexity entirely through natural oxidation - no fortification spirits added. Among the world's most distinctive and debated fortified-style wines.

Cuvée page →

Morgante Don Antonio

4.5
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · Medium acidity · Bold · Contemplative

MorganteSicily€€€€€€€Still Red

Don Antonio is Morgante's single-vineyard selection: the richest, most concentrated Nero d'Avola from the estate with barrique aging producing a wine of chocolate-dark-fruit depth and silky structure.

Cuvée page →

Murgo Brut Metodo Classico

4.7
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Light-bodied · Low tannin · Racy acidity · Festive · Elegant

MurgoSicily€€€€€€€Sparkling Traditional

Sicily's most celebrated traditional-method sparkling wine: pure Nerello Mascalese fermented in bottle and aged 36-plus months on lees, producing fine bubbles, red-berry freshness and volcanic mineral character.

Cuvée page →

Occhipinti Bombolieri

★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · High acidity · Contemplative · Elegant

Arianna OcchipintiSicily€€€€€€€€Still Red

Occhipinti's top-tier DOCG from the Bombolieri single vineyard: equal parts Frappato and Nero d'Avola in their most complex and age-worthy expression, mineral-driven with remarkable persistence.

Cuvée page →

Occhipinti SP68 Rosso

4.8
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Light-bodied · Low tannin · Racy acidity · Refreshing · Funky

Arianna OcchipintiSicily€€€€€€€Still Red

Named after the SP68 road through Vittoria, Occhipinti's signature blend is a Frappato-dominant light red of extraordinary mineral lift, wild-berry purity and racy acidity.

Cuvée page →

Passopisciaro Contrada R (Rampante)

★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Firm tannin · High acidity · Contemplative · Bold

Passopisciaro (Vini Franchetti)Sicily€€€€€€€€Still Red

Contrada R Rampante, from the highest-altitude Passopisciaro site at 1,000 metres, produces the most structured and slow-evolving wine of the range: taut, mineral and built for exceptional longevity.

Cuvée page →

Passopisciaro Franchetti

4.9
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · High acidity · Bold · Contemplative

Passopisciaro (Vini Franchetti)Sicily€€€€€€€€€Still Red

Franchetti is the prestige cuvee of the estate: a powerful blend of Petit Verdot and Cesanese d'Affile from Etna's volcanic soils, combining Bordeaux structure with the island's volcanic energy. A wine of exceptional concentration and longevity.

Cuvée page →

Pietradolce Archineri Etna Bianco

★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Low tannin · Racy acidity · Elegant · Contemplative

PietradolceSicily€€€€€€€€Still White

Archineri is Pietradolce's benchmark Etna Bianco Superiore: pure Carricante of nervy mineral tension, citrus brightness and the chalky volcanic finesse that defines Milo-area white wines.

Pietradolce Vigna Barbagalli

★★★★★★★★★★

PietradolceSicily€€€€€€€€€Still Red

Vigna Barbagalli is Pietradolce's prestige single-vineyard Etna Rosso from ungrafted pre-phylloxera vines at 850 metres: among the most collectible and age-worthy Nerello Mascalese produced on the north slope.

Planeta Burdese

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · Medium acidity · Elegant · Contemplative

PlanetaSicily€€€€€€€€Still Red

Burdese is Planeta's prestige Cabernet Sauvignon-Franc blend, named in Sicilian dialect for Bordeaux. Grown at Ulmo in Menfi, the wine achieves the concentration of ripeness that Sicily's sun provides while preserving the variety's structural backbone through altitude and careful vinification.

Cuvée page →

Planeta Cerasuolo di Vittoria

4.4
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · High acidity · Rustic · Savoury

Planeta Dorilli (Vittoria)Sicily€€€€€€€Still Red

Planeta's DOCG expression from the Dorilli contrada in Vittoria: classic cherry-sour-herb character of the appellation with the polish and consistency Planeta's winemaking team delivers across all estates.

Cuvée page →

Planeta Chardonnay

4.6
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Low tannin · Medium acidity · Elegant · Bold

PlanetaSicily€€€€€€€Still White

Planeta's barrique-aged Chardonnay from Ulmo at Menfi became one of the wines that announced Sicily's quality transformation in the 1990s: rich, buttery, confident, with Sicilian sunshine layered through Burgundian oak treatment.

Cuvée page →

Planeta Cometa

4.7
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Low tannin · High acidity · Elegant · Savoury

PlanetaSicily€€€€€€€Still White

Cometa is Sicily's signature Fiano: Planeta introduced Campanian Fiano to Menfi soils and produced a wine distinct from Southern Italian Fiano with a mineral-salt and almond-toasted character specific to the Mediterranean coast.

Cuvée page →

Planeta Santa Cecilia

4.7
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · Medium acidity · Bold · Contemplative

PlanetaSicily€€€€€€€Still Red

Santa Cecilia is Planeta's prestige single-estate Nero d'Avola from Noto: rich, structured and mineral with chocolate-dark-fruit depth and the long finish that defines this appellation.

Cuvée page →

Tasca d'Almerita Capofaro Malvasia delle Lipari

4.7
★★★★★★★★★★

Sweet · Full-bodied · Low tannin · Medium acidity · Festive · Romantic

Tasca d'Almerita Capofaro (Salina)Sicily€€€€€€€€Dessert Passito

Capofaro's Malvasia delle Lipari Passito is produced at one of Italy's most dramatically situated wineries, with terrace vines overlooking Stromboli. The Tasca d'Almerita winemaking precision elevates the passito style with clean, precise floral-honeyed concentration and the distinctive volcanic mineral signature of Salina.

Cuvée page →

Tasca d'Almerita Chardonnay

4.4
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Low tannin · Medium acidity · Elegant · Bold

Tasca d'AlmeritaSicily€€€€€€€Still White

Tasca's Chardonnay from the high Regaleali plateau shows what Sicilian sun and altitude do to Chardonnay: richer and more tropical than Burgundy but retaining the Regaleali site's mineral backbone.

Cuvée page →

Tasca d'Almerita Nozze d'Oro

4.4
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Low tannin · High acidity · Elegant · Refreshing

Tasca d'AlmeritaSicily€€€€€€€Still White

Created for the fiftieth anniversary of Lucio and Franca Tasca's wedding, Nozze d'Oro pairs Inzolia's stone-fruit softness with Sauvignon Blanc's aromatic herbal freshness in an enduring premium Sicilian white.

Cuvée page →

Tasca d'Almerita Regaleali Bianco

4.2
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Low tannin · High acidity · Refreshing · Easygoing

Tasca d'AlmeritaSicily€€€€€€Still White

Sicily's most widely distributed native-white blend: Regaleali Bianco is a reliable, aromatic white of citrus and stone fruit freshness from the island's historic Tasca estate.

Cuvée page →

Tasca d'Almerita Rosso del Conte

4.8
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · High tannin · High acidity · Contemplative · Elegant

Tasca d'AlmeritaSicily€€€€€€€€Still Red

Sicily's most historic prestige red: Tasca's Rosso del Conte blends Nero d'Avola with Perricone from the Regaleali plateau, aged two years in large oak. It is the island's longest-established benchmark for structured, age-worthy Sicilian reds.

Cuvée page →

Tenuta delle Terre Nere Calderara Sottana

4.8
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Firm tannin · High acidity · Contemplative · Elegant

Tenuta delle Terre NereSicily€€€€€€€€Still Red

Calderara Sottana is a benchmark for north-slope Etna Rosso: Burgundian elegance with volcanic minerality, tart red-fruit purity and the structural firmness that demands 8-plus years.

Cuvée page →

Tenuta delle Terre Nere Guardiola

4.9
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Firm tannin · High acidity · Contemplative · Bold

Tenuta delle Terre NereSicily€€€€€€€€Still Red

The Guardiola contrada yields the most powerful and structured of the Terre Nere single-vineyard range: darker fruit, greater density, and a long mineral-chalky finish.

★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · Medium acidity · Elegant · Bold

Zisola (Mazzei)Sicily€€€€€€€Still Red

Doppiozeta is Zisola's flagship Nero d'Avola from Noto DOC: Mazzei's Tuscan winemaking precision applied to Sicilian Nero d'Avola produces a wine of elegant structure and tobacco-mineral depth.

★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · High acidity · Elegant · Savoury

Tenuta di CapezzanaTuscany€€€€€€€Still Red

Capezzana's flagship Carmignano DOCG, Sangiovese-Cabernet from the original Tuscan appellation to allow Cabernet (since the 18th century).

Cuvée page →

Adami Vigneto Giardino Rive di Colbertaldo Valdobbiadene Prosecco Superiore Brut

4.6
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Light-bodied · High acidity · Elegant · Savoury

AdamiVeneto€€€€€€€Sparkling Tank

One of the most admired single-vineyard Rive expressions in the Conegliano Valdobbiadene DOCG. The Giardino parcel sits on the steep Colbertaldo hillside; steep gradient, poor soils and attentive pruning give the wine its notable concentration and precision.

Cuvée page →

Allegrini Amarone della Valpolicella Classico

4.7
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · High acidity · Elegant · Bold

AllegriniVeneto€€€€€€€€€Dessert Passito

Allegrini's estate Amarone Classico blends Corvina and Corvinone in nearly equal measure with a small percentage of Oseleta; the result is a polished, internationally styled Amarone with plush dried-fruit concentration and a refined tannin frame.

Cuvée page →

Allegrini Amarone della Valpolicella Classico Fieramonte

4.9
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · High acidity · Contemplative · Elegant

AllegriniVeneto€€€€€€€€€€Dessert Passito

The single-vineyard Fieramonte takes Allegrini's Amarone to the highest level: a single hill block in the Classico zone with extreme concentration, the Oseleta component adding signature pepper warmth to the cedar-espresso complex.

Cuvée page →

Allegrini La Poja

4.8
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · High acidity · Bold · Elegant

AllegriniVeneto€€€€€€€€€Still Red

A 100% Corvina IGT aged in French barriques rather than dried: La Poja demonstrates the varietal's structure and ageing potential when treated outside the appassimento framework, producing a powerful, savoury red that rivals Amarone in complexity.

Cuvée page →

Bertani Amarone della Valpolicella Classico

4.8
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · High acidity · Contemplative · Elegant

BertaniVeneto€€€€€€€€€Dessert Passito

Bertani's Amarone is the reference for the traditional, extremely long-aged style: minimum eight years in large Slavonian oak produces a garrigue-herb and leather-inflected wine of remarkable freshness and longevity, lighter in colour than modern Amarone but strikingly complex.

Cuvée page →

Bertani Amarone della Valpolicella Villa Novare

4.9
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · High acidity · Contemplative · Elegant

BertaniVeneto€€€€€€€€€Dessert Passito

Bertani's Villa Novare is the prestige Amarone from their historic Novare estate, given six years in Slavonian oak producing a wine of extraordinary complexity and longevity; perhaps the most authentically traditional Amarone Riserva-style bottling in the appellation.

Cuvée page →

Bisol Cartizze Valdobbiadene Superiore di Cartizze

4.6
★★★★★★★★★★

Off Dry · Light-bodied · Medium acidity · Festive · Elegant

Bisol 1542Veneto€€€€€€€€Sparkling Tank

Rich and aromatic expression from the 107-ha Cartizze grand cru hill. Bisol's long tenure on the hill and multi-generational vine stock give this off-dry sparkler a layered complexity unusual in Prosecco.

Cuvée page →

Bortolomiol Senior Valdobbiadene Prosecco Superiore Extra Dry

4.2
★★★★★★★★★★

Off Dry · Light-bodied · Medium acidity · Easygoing · Refreshing

BortolomiolVeneto€€€€€€Sparkling Tank

The flagship Extra Dry from Bortolomiol, founded in 1949 by Giuliano Bortolomiol. Named 'Senior' in tribute to the founder, this is a classic off-dry Valdobbiadene DOCG with the balance of sweetness and freshness that made the Extra Dry category popular.

Cuvée page →

Cantine Campagnola Amarone della Valpolicella Classico

4.2
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · High acidity · Bold · Contemplative

Cantine CampagnolaVeneto€€€€€€€€Dessert Passito

Campagnola's Amarone della Valpolicella Classico is produced from appassimento-dried Corvina, Corvinone and Rondinella grapes from the Classico hills. Two to three years in large Slavonian oak develop the characteristic dried fruit, dark chocolate and tobacco complexity of the DOCG's flagship wine.

Cuvée page →

Col Vetoraz Valdobbiadene Superiore di Cartizze

4.6
★★★★★★★★★★

Off Dry · Light-bodied · Medium acidity · Festive · Elegant

Col Vetoraz SpumantiVeneto€€€€€€€€Sparkling Tank

Col Vetoraz is uniquely positioned above Santo Stefano at the edge of the Cartizze hill, giving its Cartizze expression exceptional altitude and south-facing exposure. The result is an opulent, aromatic Cartizze with great aromatic intensity and length.

Cuvée page →

Corte Sant'Alda Amarone della Valpolicella Mithas

4.7
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · High acidity · Contemplative · Funky

Corte Sant'AldaVeneto€€€€€€€€€Dessert Passito

Made by Demeter-certified biodynamic winemaker Marinella Camerani, Mithas is among the most distinctive Amarone: Demeter-certified estate viticulture produces a wine of unusual earthy complexity, mineral precision and high acidity that evolves beautifully with bottle age.

Cuvée page →

Dal Forno Romano Amarone della Valpolicella

5.0
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · High acidity · Contemplative · Bold

Dal Forno RomanoVeneto€€€€€€€€€€Dessert Passito

The most powerful and extracted Amarone on the market: tiny yields, four-month drying, four years in small French oak. Immense concentration of dried fruit, dark chocolate and iron. Built for multi-decade cellaring.

Cuvée page →

Dal Forno Romano Recioto della Valpolicella

4.9
★★★★★★★★★★

Sweet · Full-bodied · Medium tannin · Medium acidity · Romantic · Contemplative

Dal Forno RomanoVeneto€€€€€€€€€€Dessert Passito

Dal Forno's sweet Recioto applies the same barrique-and-concentration philosophy: deep, almost port-like sweetness balanced by the estate's signature tannin structure.

Cuvée page →

Dal Forno Romano Valpolicella Superiore

4.9
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · High acidity · Bold · Contemplative

Dal Forno RomanoVeneto€€€€€€€€€Still Red

Dal Forno's entry into the Valpolicella range already shows the estate's signature intensity: deeply coloured, barrique-aged and concentrated beyond what the DOC classification implies.

Cuvée page →

Gini Soave Classico La Frosca

4.7
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Low tannin · Racy acidity · Elegant · Refreshing

GiniVeneto€€€€€€€Still White

La Frosca from the Gini family's oldest Garganega vines on the highest basalt-volcanic slopes of Monteforte d'Alpone; the chalky mineral texture, fennel herb and white-peach fruit represent the Classico zone's basalt terroir in its purest expression.

Cuvée page →

Giuseppe Quintarelli Alzero

5.0
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · High acidity · Contemplative · Bold

Giuseppe QuintarelliVeneto€€€€€€€€€€Dessert Passito

Quintarelli's Bordeaux varieties treated to the Valpolicella appassimento method: dried Cabernet and Merlot producing extraordinary density, graphite-inflected fruit and a seamlessly long finish.

Cuvée page →

Giuseppe Quintarelli Amarone della Valpolicella Classico

5.0
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · High acidity · Contemplative · Elegant

Giuseppe QuintarelliVeneto€€€€€€€€€€Dessert Passito

The definitive benchmark Amarone: monumental concentration from multi-variety appassimento, extraordinary aromatic depth of dried fruit, leather and tobacco, with firm but resolved tannins and a finish of exceptional length.

Cuvée page →

Giuseppe Quintarelli Primofiore

4.8
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · High acidity · Bold · Contemplative

Giuseppe QuintarelliVeneto€€€€€€€€€Still Red

A blend of local Corvina with international Cabernet in the Quintarelli style: concentrated, savoury and structured, with the Bordeaux varieties adding colour and spine to the Valpolicella base.

Cuvée page →

Giuseppe Quintarelli Recioto della Valpolicella Classico

4.9
★★★★★★★★★★

Sweet · Full-bodied · Medium tannin · Medium acidity · Contemplative · Romantic

Giuseppe QuintarelliVeneto€€€€€€€€€€Dessert Passito

The sweet passito counterpart to Quintarelli's Amarone: residual sugar balanced by firm tannins and high acidity producing a luscious yet structured dessert red with decades-long ageing potential.

Cuvée page →

Giuseppe Quintarelli Valpolicella Classico Superiore

4.7
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · High acidity · Rustic · Savoury

Giuseppe QuintarelliVeneto€€€€€€€€€Still Red

Quintarelli's entry-level Valpolicella is already a lesson in traditional winemaking: high-acid, savoury cherry fruit with dried-herb complexity and three years in large oak adding gentle structure.

Cuvée page →

Guerrieri Rizzardi Pojega Bardolino Superiore

4.3
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · High acidity · Rustic · Savoury

Guerrieri RizzardiVeneto€€€€€€€Still Red

Pojega is the top expression from Guerrieri Rizzardi's Bardolino range, bottled under the Bardolino Superiore DOCG. Oak ageing adds structure and complexity to the base Classico profile. Named for the Pojega wine museum located on the estate.

Cuvée page →

Guerrieri Rizzardi Villa Rizzardi Amarone della Valpolicella Classico

4.4
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · High tannin · High acidity · Contemplative · Bold

Guerrieri RizzardiVeneto€€€€€€€€Dessert Passito

Guerrieri Rizzardi's Amarone expresses the Classico zone from an estate with over 350 years of Valpolicella history. The extended appassimento (drying) of Corvina and Corvinone grapes concentrates flavour into a wine of great intensity. Three years in Slavonian botti soften the tannins while preserving the dried-fruit character.

Cuvée page →

Inama Bradisismo Soave Superiore DOCG

4.9
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Low tannin · High acidity · Elegant · Contemplative

Inama Azienda AgricolaVeneto€€€€€€€€Still White

Bradisismo (Italian for seismic land subsidence) is Inama's prestige selection from the oldest basalt-grown Garganega vines, aged 18-24 months in French barriques; arguably the most complex Soave available, with extraordinary tension between rich oak ageing and the grape's saline mineral core.

Cuvée page →

Inama Soave Classico Superiore Vigneti di Foscarino

4.7
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Low tannin · Racy acidity · Elegant · Refreshing

Inama Azienda AgricolaVeneto€€€€€€€€Still White

Vigneti di Foscarino is Inama's pure basalt-volcanic Soave Classico from the historic Foscarino hill, the most volcanically active terroir in the Classico zone; 100% Garganega barrel-fermented for a mineral, almond-hazelnut complexity of striking length.

Cuvée page →

Lorenzo Begali Amarone della Valpolicella Classico Monte Ca' Bianca

4.7
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · High acidity · Contemplative · Rustic

Lorenzo BegaliVeneto€€€€€€€€€Dessert Passito

Begali's Monte Ca' Bianca is a traditional-school Amarone from very old Corvina vines; the deep mineral iron note and high acidity set it apart, making it one of the most distinctive and food-friendly examples in the Classico zone.

Cuvée page →

Maculan Acininobili Breganze

4.9
★★★★★★★★★★

Dessert · Full-bodied · High acidity · Contemplative · Elegant

MaculanVeneto€€€€€€€€€Dessert Noble Rot

One of Italy's greatest botrytised dessert wines. Acininobili (noble berries) is produced from 100% botrytis-affected Vespaiola grapes, selected berry by berry in the best vintages. The Sauternes influence is explicit: Maculan was inspired by a trip to Bordeaux in the late 1970s and applied the Trockenbeerenauslese selection principle to Vespaiola.

Cuvée page →

Maculan Fratta Breganze Rosso

4.7
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · High tannin · High acidity · Contemplative · Bold

MaculanVeneto€€€€€€€€Still Red

Fratta is Maculan's Bordeaux-inspired flagship red, one of northeastern Italy's landmark wines. A Cabernet-Merlot blend aged 18 months in new French oak, it rivals top Veneto reds in complexity and ageing potential. Fausto Maculan was among the first producers in the Veneto to vinify Bordeaux varieties at serious quality levels.

Cuvée page →

Maculan Torcolato Breganze

4.8
★★★★★★★★★★

Sweet · Full-bodied · High acidity · Contemplative · Elegant

MaculanVeneto€€€€€€€€Dessert Passito

Italy's most celebrated passito-style dessert wine from Vicenza. Vespaiola grapes are harvested late and dried on twisted cords (torcolato means 'twisted') for months before pressing. The result is a wine of remarkable intensity, with botrytis-influenced apricot honey character balanced by the grape's naturally high acidity.

Cuvée page →

Masi Campofiorin Rosso del Veronese

4.4
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · Medium acidity · Rustic · Easygoing

Masi AgricolaVeneto€€€€€€€Still Red

Campofiorin pioneered the ripasso technique in 1964, passing fresh Valpolicella wine over dried Amarone grape skins to extract colour, richness and complexity. A seminal wine that created an entire style category.

Cuvée page →

Masi Campolongo di Torbe Amarone della Valpolicella Classico

4.8
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · High acidity · Contemplative · Bold

Masi AgricolaVeneto€€€€€€€€€Dessert Passito

From the single Torbe hillside vineyard in the Negrar valley, Campolongo di Torbe is characterised by spicy, tobacco-inflected dried fruit with earthier notes than Masi's more opulent Mazzano bottling.

Cuvée page →

Masi Costasera Amarone della Valpolicella Classico

4.5
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · High acidity · Rustic · Bold

Masi AgricolaVeneto€€€€€€€€Dessert Passito

Masi's entry-level Classico Amarone is the house's most widely distributed wine and among the most accessible classic-zone bottlings, balancing dried-cherry concentration with a restrained herbaceous character and good structure.

Cuvée page →

Masi Mazzano Amarone della Valpolicella Classico

4.9
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · High acidity · Contemplative · Bold

Masi AgricolaVeneto€€€€€€€€€Dessert Passito

Mazzano is Masi's most powerful and concentrated single-vineyard Amarone, from the high-altitude Mazzano hill in the S. Ambrogio zone. More opulent and riper than Campolongo, with a chocolatey, rich palate and remarkable ageing potential.

Cuvée page →

Masi Serego Alighieri Vaio Armaron Amarone della Valpolicella Classico

4.7
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · High acidity · Contemplative · Elegant

Masi AgricolaVeneto€€€€€€€€€Dessert Passito

Vaio Armaron is the prestige Amarone from the historic Serego Alighieri estate, owned by descendants of Dante Alighieri, managed in partnership with Masi; the wine reflects the S. Ambrogio di Valpolicella hill terroir with a refined tobacco-cedar complexity and long cellaring potential.

Cuvée page →

Nino Franco Grave di Stecca Valdobbiadene Superiore di Cartizze

4.6
★★★★★★★★★★

Off Dry · Light-bodied · High acidity · Elegant · Festive

Nino FrancoVeneto€€€€€€€€Sparkling Tank

A single-hill Cartizze grand cru expression from the Stecca parcel, with richer fruit, honeyed florality and greater complexity than the standard Rustico, yet retaining the hallmark lightness of the hill.

Cuvée page →

Nino Franco Rustico Valdobbiadene Prosecco Superiore

4.3
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Light-bodied · High acidity · Refreshing · Festive

Nino FrancoVeneto€€€€€€Sparkling Tank

The benchmark entry-level Prosecco Superiore DOCG from Nino Franco. Pale straw with fine persistent perlage; white stone fruit and floral notes lead to a crisp, dry finish.

Cuvée page →

Ottella Molceo Lugana Riserva DOC

4.6
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · High acidity · Elegant · Contemplative

OttellaVeneto€€€€€€€Still White

Molceo is among the most complex white wines produced in the Lugana DOC. Extended lees contact in both oak and steel develops remarkable textural richness and autolytic complexity in the Turbiana grape. One of the first producers to prove that Lugana could produce serious age-worthy whites.

Cuvée page →

Pieropan Le Colombare Recioto di Soave

4.8
★★★★★★★★★★

Sweet · Medium-bodied · Low tannin · High acidity · Romantic · Elegant

PieropanVeneto€€€€€€€€Dessert Passito

Pieropan's Recioto di Soave from the Le Colombare parcel is the most prestigious expression of the ancient sweet white wine tradition of the Soave zone; dried Garganega produces extraordinary apricot-honey concentration kept fresh by the varietal's trademark high acidity.

Cuvée page →

Pieropan Soave Classico

4.6
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Light-bodied · Low tannin · High acidity · Refreshing · Easygoing

PieropanVeneto€€€€€€Still White

Pieropan's estate Soave Classico is the reference-standard expression of the appellation: Garganega's characteristic almond-blossom and white-peach aromas with a saline, mineral finish from the volcanic basalt hillside soils of the Classico zone.

Cuvée page →

Pieropan Soave Classico Calvarino

4.9
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Low tannin · Racy acidity · Elegant · Refreshing

PieropanVeneto€€€€€€€Still White

Calvarino is Pieropan's basalt and limestone single-vineyard Soave, planted in 1924 to Garganega and Trebbiano di Soave; the volcanic basalt soils give a crackling acidity and mineral depth far beyond the appellation average, with exceptional ageing potential.

Cuvée page →

Pieropan Soave Classico La Rocca

5.0
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Low tannin · High acidity · Elegant · Contemplative

PieropanVeneto€€€€€€€€Still White

La Rocca is 100% Garganega from the volcanic tufa hillside above Soave castle, barrel-fermented and aged; arguably Italy's most cellar-worthy white wine, developing hazelnut-honey complexity over 10-plus years while retaining the grape's almond-mineral core.

Cuvée page →

Pra Monte Grande Soave Classico

4.7
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Low tannin · Racy acidity · Elegant · Refreshing

PraVeneto€€€€€€€Still White

Pra's Monte Grande from the basalt-rich Monteforte hillside is a benchmark organic Soave Classico: Graziano Pra's organic viticulture and extended lees contact produce a pure, mineral, textural wine of genuine complexity and age-worthiness for the price.

Cuvée page →

Roberto Anselmi Capitel Croce

4.6
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Low tannin · High acidity · Elegant · Contemplative

Roberto AnselmiVeneto€€€€€€€€Still White

Capitel Croce is Anselmi's barrel-aged 100% Garganega, named for a wayside cross on the Capitel Croce hill; oak-fermented with extended lees contact to produce a richer, more complex expression of the varietal with hazelnuts, golden apple and creamy texture.

Cuvée page →

Roberto Anselmi I Capitelli

4.7
★★★★★★★★★★

Sweet · Medium-bodied · Low tannin · High acidity · Romantic · Contemplative

Roberto AnselmiVeneto€€€€€€€€Dessert Late Harvest

I Capitelli is Anselmi's late-harvest sweet Garganega, made from overripe and botrytised bunches from the Capitel Croce and Capitel Foscarino hills; rich, honeyed and apricot-dominated but lifted by the grape's natural acidity that keeps it from cloying.

Cuvée page →

Roberto Anselmi San Vincenzo

4.4
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Light-bodied · Low tannin · High acidity · Refreshing · Easygoing

Roberto AnselmiVeneto€€€€€€Still White

San Vincenzo is Anselmi's most widely distributed wine, a Garganega-Chardonnay Veneto IGT that captures the almond-blossom freshness of the Soave zone in a light, food-friendly, affordable white. The Chardonnay adds body without altering the Garganega character.

Cuvée page →

Ruggeri Cartizze Valdobbiadene Superiore di Cartizze

4.4
★★★★★★★★★★

Off Dry · Light-bodied · Medium acidity · Festive · Elegant

Ruggeri and C.Veneto€€€€€€€Sparkling Tank

Ruggeri's Cartizze expression from the storied 107-hectare grand cru hill. Off-dry and aromatic with richer fruit and honey than the standard Valdobbiadene range, showing the concentration that the south-facing Cartizze slopes produce.

Cuvée page →

Ruggeri Giustino B Valdobbiadene Prosecco Superiore Extra Dry

4.3
★★★★★★★★★★

Off Dry · Light-bodied · Medium acidity · Easygoing · Refreshing

Ruggeri and C.Veneto€€€€€€Sparkling Tank

The historical benchmark of Ruggeri, first produced when the house was founded in 1950. Giustino B is named after Giustino Bisol, who co-founded the house; this Extra Dry expression became one of the most recognised Prosecco Superiore labels in the zone.

Cuvée page →

Serafini and Vidotto Il Rosso dell'Abazia

4.6
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · High acidity · Contemplative · Elegant

Serafini and VidottoVeneto€€€€€€€€Still Red

The flagship of the Montello DOCG, one of northeastern Italy's most serious Bordeaux-blend reds. Serafini and Vidotto pioneered quality Bordeaux-variety production on the Montello hills in the 1980s when the zone was considered secondary. Il Rosso dell'Abazia has become the reference point for the designation.

Cuvée page →

Speri Amarone della Valpolicella Classico

4.6
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · High acidity · Rustic · Elegant

Speri ViticoltoriVeneto€€€€€€€€Dessert Passito

Speri's estate Amarone Classico reflects the six-generation family's traditional approach with large-cask ageing, producing a fresh, floral, tobacco-inflected Amarone that remains one of the most consistent classic-zone expressions.

Cuvée page →

Speri Amarone della Valpolicella Classico Vigneto Monte Sant'Urbano

4.8
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · High acidity · Contemplative · Elegant

Speri ViticoltoriVeneto€€€€€€€€€Dessert Passito

Monte Sant'Urbano is Speri's flagship single-vineyard Amarone from the historic cru on the Fumane hillside; traditional large-cask ageing preserves freshness alongside the dried-fruit concentration, producing one of the Classico zone's most elegant and age-worthy Amarone.

Cuvée page →

Suavia Soave Classico Monte Carbonare

4.8
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Low tannin · Racy acidity · Elegant · Refreshing

SuaviaVeneto€€€€€€€€Still White

Suavia's Monte Carbonare from the village of Soave itself: 100% Garganega from a steep volcanic tufa hillside with century-old vines; lees-aged in large casks producing exceptional mineral depth, citrus-almond precision and great cellaring potential.

Cuvée page →

Tedeschi Amarone della Valpolicella Classico Capitel Monte Olmi

4.8
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · High acidity · Contemplative · Elegant

Tedeschi ViniVeneto€€€€€€€€€Dessert Passito

Capitel Monte Olmi is Tedeschi's single-vineyard prestige Amarone, from the Monte Olmi hill above San Pietro in Cariano, with exceptional concentration and finesse, reflecting one of the oldest family estates in Valpolicella.

Cuvée page →

Tenuta Sant'Antonio Amarone della Valpolicella Campo dei Gigli

4.6
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · High acidity · Bold · Rustic

Tenuta Sant'AntonioVeneto€€€€€€€€€Dessert Passito

The 20% Oseleta in Campo dei Gigli adds a peppery, dark-spice dimension to the Corvina-Corvinone dried fruit base; from the Mezzane valley outside the Classico zone, the wine shows an earthier, more structured style than comparable Classico Amarone.

Cuvée page →

Tommasi Amarone della Valpolicella Classico

4.5
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · High acidity · Rustic · Bold

Tommasi ViticoltoriVeneto€€€€€€€€Dessert Passito

Tommasi's estate Amarone Classico represents the fourth-generation family's traditional approach: a high proportion of Corvinone adds floral lift to the dried-cherry concentration while three years in large casks develops structure.

Cuvée page →

Tommasi Amarone della Valpolicella Classico Ca' Florian

4.7
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · High acidity · Contemplative · Bold

Tommasi ViticoltoriVeneto€€€€€€€€€Dessert Passito

Ca' Florian is Tommasi's single-vineyard flagship Amarone from the old Ca' Florian hillside in the Classico zone, showing dense, chocolatey concentration with elegant tannin structure and long mineral finish.

Cuvée page →

Vignalta Colli Euganei Fior d'Arancio Passito

4.7
★★★★★★★★★★

Sweet · Full-bodied · High acidity · Contemplative · Elegant

VignaltaVeneto€€€€€€€Dessert Passito

The defining wine of the Colli Euganei Fior d'Arancio DOCG, Italy's most aromatic dessert wine appellation. Moscato Giallo grapes are dried (passito) on bamboo racks in the volcanic Euganean Hills; the intensely floral and orange-scented result is unlike any other Italian sweet wine.

Cuvée page →

Zenato Amarone della Valpolicella Classico

4.5
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · High acidity · Bold · Rustic

ZenatoVeneto€€€€€€€€Dessert Passito

Zenato's Classico Amarone incorporates 20% Oseleta adding firm pepper tannin and dark fruit spice to the Corvina-led blend; aged in a combination of large Slavonian and small French oak that bridges traditional and modern styles.

Cuvée page →

Zenato Amarone della Valpolicella Classico Riserva Sergio Zenato

4.9
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · High acidity · Contemplative · Elegant

ZenatoVeneto€€€€€€€€€Dessert Passito

The Riserva Sergio Zenato is the estate's memorial bottling to its founder, made only in exceptional vintages and aged five years in oak; the highest Oseleta proportion and extended ageing produce Zenato's most structured and concentrated wine, with extraordinary longevity.

Cuvée page →

Zyme Amarone della Valpolicella Classico Kairos

4.7
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · High acidity · Bold · Elegant

ZymeVeneto€€€€€€€€€Dessert Passito

Zyme's Kairos is a modern interpretation of Classico Amarone; former Quintarelli cellarmaster Celestino Gaspari applies small French oak ageing to produce a polished, concentrated expression that bridges traditional intensity with contemporary oak integration.

Cuvée page →

Weingut F.X. PichlerWachau€€€€€€€€Still White

Richer, more luxuriant Smaragd Grüner Veltliner from the Frauenweingärten (women's vineyards) Ried: concentrated peach and pepper spice with full body and mineral depth.
